tools.name: list_pulseem_operations
tools.description:
Returns live list of ALL available Pulseem operations (from the live Swagger spec).
Use this ONLY if you need to verify exact operationIds before calling CallPulseemApi.

tools.name: call_pulseem_api
tools.description:
Executes any Pulseem API operation.
BEFORE CALLING: Always call GetPulseemInstructions first.

operationId (case-insensitive):
  SendSms, GetSMSReport, GetIncomingSmsReport,
  SendEmail, GetEmailReport,
  SendWhatsAppChat, SendWhatsAppTemplate, SubmitWhatsAppTemplate,
  GetTemplateStatus, GetWhatsAp

tools.name: get_pulseem_instructions
tools.description:
⚠️ ALWAYS call this tool FIRST before any other Pulseem tool.

Returns:
- Date/time format rules (CRITICAL — wrong format = server error)
- Required fields for every operation type
- Full JSON examples for SendSms, SendEmail, SendWhatsAppChat, SendWhatsAppTemplate
- All error codes and their meaning

tools.name: get_pulseem_api_docs
tools.description:
Returns the FULL detailed Pulseem API documentation for a specific category.
category: 'sms' | 'email' | 'whatsapp' | 'accounts' | 'clients' | 'all'

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "list_pulseem_operations" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Returns live list of ALL available Pulseem operations (from the live Swagger spec). Use this ONLY if you need to verify exact operationIds before calling CallPu…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "call_pulseem_api" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Executes any Pulseem API operation. BEFORE CALLING: Always call GetPulseemInstructions first. operationId (case-insensitive): SendSms, GetSMSReport, GetIncoming…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_pulseem_api_docs · Public sink: get_pulseem_api_docs · Destructive: call_pulseem_api, get_pulseem_instructions.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_pulseem_api_docs · Public sink: get_pulseem_api_docs · Destructive: call_pulseem_api, get_pulseem_instructions

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "get_pulseem_api_docs" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet:
before: "(hash 3afc3af3)" → after: "Returns the FULL detailed Pulseem API documentation for a specific category.
category: 'sms' | 'email' | 'whatsapp' | 'accounts' | 'clients' | 'all'"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
